cb_step_1.title: Select CT Volume
cb_step_1.instruction: Choose the CT volume from the list.

cb_step_2.title: Create Reference Segmentation
cb_step_2.instruction: Switch to the Segment Editor module and create a new segmentation named "Reference_Segmentation".

cb_step_3.title: Add and Rename Segment
cb_step_3.instruction: Click Add and rename the segment to "Reference_Segment".

cb_step_5.title: Adjust Threshold Range
cb_step_5.instruction: Set the threshold slider to the desired range.

cb_step_9.title: Select Reference Part
cb_step_9.instruction: Click the reference part island in the 2D view.

cb_step_11.title: Create Moving Segmentation
cb_step_11.instruction: Create a new segmentation named "Moving_Segmentation" in the Segment Editor.

cb_step_12.title: Add and Rename Moving Segment
cb_step_12.instruction: Click Add and rename the segment to "Moving_Segment".

cb_step_14.title: Adjust Moving Threshold Range
cb_step_14.instruction: Set the threshold slider to the desired range.

cb_step_18.title: Select Moving Part
cb_step_18.instruction: Click the moving part island in the 2D view.

cb_step_20.title: Select Reference Segmentation
cb_step_20.instruction: Choose the reference segmentation node from the dropdown.

cb_step_22.title: Select Moving Segmentation
cb_step_22.instruction: Choose the moving segmentation node from the dropdown.

cb_step_25.title: Adjust Reference ROI
cb_step_25.instruction: Drag the ROI handles to enclose the fractured part of the reference model.

cb_step_27.title: Adjust Moving ROI
cb_step_27.instruction: Drag the ROI handles to enclose the fractured part of the moving model.

cb_step_30.title: Manual Adjustment Needed?
cb_step_30.instruction: Check the box if you want to manually adjust the moving fragment.

cb_step_31.title: Adjust Camera View
cb_step_31.instruction: Rotate and zoom the 3D view to inspect the moving fragment.

operation_descriptions: Create a new segmentation named 'Reference_Segmentation'.
Add a segment named 'Reference_Segment' to the reference segmentation.
Activate the Threshold effect in Segment Editor.
Apply the threshold effect.
Activate the Islands effect in Segment Editor.
Set the Islands effect to 'Keep selected island' option.
Set the reference segment invisible.
Create a new segmentation named 'Moving_Segmentation'.
Add a segment named 'Moving_Segment' to the moving segmentation.
Activate the Threshold effect for moving segmentation.
Apply the threshold effect for moving segmentation.
Activate the Islands effect for moving segmentation.
Set the Islands effect to 'Keep selected island' for moving.
Set the moving segment invisible.
Create a new segmentation named 'Reference_Segmentation'.
Add a segment named 'Reference_Segment' to the reference segmentation.
Activate the Threshold effect in Segment Editor.
Apply the threshold effect.
Activate the Islands effect in Segment Editor.
Set the Islands effect to 'Keep selected island' option.
In the 2D view, click to select the reference part.
Set the reference segment invisible.
Create a new segmentation named 'Moving_Segmentation'.
Add a segment named 'Moving_Segment' to the moving segmentation.
Activate the Threshold effect for moving segmentation.
Apply the threshold effect for moving segmentation.
Activate the Islands effect for moving segmentation.
Set the Islands effect to 'Keep selected island' for moving.
In the 2D view, click to select the moving part.
Set the moving segment invisible.
Perform 3D reconstruction of the reference segmentation.
Perform 3D reconstruction of the moving segmentation.
Create/reset an ROI for the reference model.
Manually adjust the boundaries of the ROI to retain the fractured part of the reference model.
Manually adjust the boundaries of the ROI to retain the fractured part of the reference model.
Create/reset an ROI for the moving model.
Manually adjust the boundaries of the ROI to retain the fractured part of the moving model.
Manually adjust the boundaries of the ROI to retain the fractured part of the moving model.
Detect fracture surfaces using reference and moving models and ROIs.
Apply fracture reduction to align the moving fragment.
Manually adjust the moving fragment.
Harden the transform into the moving fragment.

Every executable API call must use a receiver type derivable from retrieved source or wrapper evidence. Report uncertainty instead of inventing a method. Do not introduce unrelated UI, icon, toolbar, module-switching, or layout behavior.
Never fabricate values that an extension DEFINES IN ITS SOURCE — a custom-layout ID or its XML, an `slicer.<Const>` the extension sets, a registered singleton tag, or a magic constant must come from the extension's source, not be guessed. If, after searching the extension source, you cannot find such a value, emit `raise RuntimeError("MISSING_EVIDENCE: <what>")` instead of a placeholder. This does NOT apply to ordinary scene nodes referenced by name/role (a curve, segmentation, or volume the user created) — resolve those at runtime from the scene; never emit MISSING_EVIDENCE for them.

Ordinary scene nodes referenced by name are resolved at runtime, not from source.

generator_records.generated_code_preview:
# Set the reference segment invisible
# (for the LongBoneFractureReduction extension)

# Find the LongBoneFractureReduction parameter node
parameterNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass('vtkMRMLScriptedModuleNode')
if parameterNode is None or parameterNode.GetName() != 'LongBoneFractureReduction':
    raise RuntimeError("LongBoneFractureReduction parameter node not found. "
                       "Open the Long Bone Fracture Reduction module first.")

# Access the reference segmentation node via node reference
refSegNode = parameterNode.GetNodeReference('referenceSegmentation')
if refSegNode is None:
    raise RuntimeError("No reference segmentation selected. "
                       "Select a reference segmentation in the module first.")

# Ensure the display node exists
if refSegNode.GetDisplayNode() is None:
    refSegNode.CreateDefaultDisplayNodes()

displayNode = refSegNode.GetDisplayNode()

# Resolve segment ID from segment name
seg = refSegNode.GetSegmentation()
segmentId = seg.GetSegmentIdBySegmentName('Reference_Segment')
if segmentId == '':
    raise RuntimeError("Segment 'Reference_Segment' not found in reference segmentation.")

# Set the segment invisible (overall visibility: affects both 3D and 2D views)
displayNode.SetSegmentVisibility(segmentId, False)

# Verify the state was applied
if displayNode.GetSegmentVisibility(segmentId):
    raise RuntimeError("STATE_NOT_APPLIED: SetSegmentVisibility(%s, False) did not take effect." % segmentId)

# Set the reference segment invisible
# (for the LongBoneFractureReduction extension)

# Find the LongBoneFractureReduction parameter node
parameterNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass('vtkMRMLScriptedModuleNode')
if parameterNode is None or parameterNode.GetName() != 'LongBoneFractureReduction':
    raise RuntimeError("LongBoneFractureReduction parameter node not found. "
                       "Open the Long Bone Fracture Reduction module first.")

# Access the reference segmentation node via node reference
refSegNode = parameterNode.GetNodeReference('referenceSegmentation')
if refSegNode is None:
    raise RuntimeError("No reference segmentation selected. "
                       "Select a reference segmentation in the module first.")

# Ensure the display node exists
if refSegNode.GetDisplayNode() is None:
    refSegNode.CreateDefaultDisplayNodes()

displayNode = refSegNode.GetDisplayNode()

# Resolve segment ID from segment name
seg = refSegNode.GetSegmentation()
segmentId = seg.GetSegmentIdBySegmentName('Reference_Segment')
if segmentId == '':
    raise RuntimeError("Segment 'Reference_Segment' not found in reference segmentation.")

# Set the segment invisible (overall visibility: affects both 3D and 2D views)
displayNode.SetSegmentVisibility(segmentId, False)

# Verify the state was applied
if displayNode.GetSegmentVisibility(segmentId):
    raise RuntimeError("STATE_NOT_APPLIED: SetSegmentVisibility(%s, False) did not take effect." % segmentId)
